event: Bob Dylan, born Robert Zimmerman
Freddie Mercury, born Farrokh Bulsara
Elton John, born Reginald Kenneth Dwight
Madonna, born Madonna Louise Ciccone
Lady Gaga, born Stefani Joanne Angelina Germanotta
Bruno Mars, born Peter Gene Hernandez
Miley Cyrus, born Destiny Hope Cyrus
Sputnik 1 becomes the first artificial satellite
Yuri Gagarin becomes the first human in space
Valentina Tereshkova becomes the first woman in space
Alexei Leonov performs the first spacewalk
Neil Armstrong becomes the first person to walk on the moon
Salyut 1 becomes the first space station
Dennis Tito becomes the first space tourist
Monopoly – Charles Darrow patents his version of the property trading game
Scrabble – James Brunot trademarks the name for Alfred Butts' word game
Clue – Waddington's releases 'Cluedo' in the UK
Risk – Parker Brothers releases the game of global domination
Twister – Milton Bradley releases the first game using human bodies as playing pieces
Uno – Merle Robbins invents the shedding-type card game
Catan – Klaus Teuber releases 'The Settlers of Catan' in Germany
Mark Twain, born Samuel Langhorne Clemens
Lewis Carroll, born Charles Lutwidge Dodgson
George Orwell, born Eric Arthur Blair
Dr. Seuss, born Theodor Seuss Geisel
Stan Lee, born Stanley Martin Lieber
John le Carré, born David John Moore Cornwell
Lemony Snicket, born Daniel Handler
Sony Walkman – The TPS-L2 revolutionizes portable music
IBM Personal Computer – The IBM 5150 brings computing to the masses
Nintendo Entertainment System – The NES revitalizes home video gaming in North America
Game Boy – Nintendo introduces its incredibly popular handheld console
Apple iPod – '1,000 songs in your pocket' changes the music industry
Apple iPhone – The first mobile phone to use multi-touch technology
Amazon Echo – The first smart speaker featuring the Alexa voice assistant
Miracle on 34th Street – The original film starring Maureen O'Hara and Edmund Gwenn
Rudolph the Red-Nosed Reindeer – The Rankin/Bass stop-motion television special premieres
A Charlie Brown Christmas – The Peanuts gang makes their animated TV debut
A Christmas Story – Ralphie Parker begins his quest for a Red Ryder BB gun
Home Alone – Kevin McCallister defends his house from the Wet Bandits
The Nightmare Before Christmas – Jack Skellington discovers Christmas Town
Elf – Buddy the Elf travels from the North Pole to New York City
McDonald's – Founded by Richard and Maurice McDonald in San Bernardino
In-N-Out Burger – California's first drive-thru hamburger stand opens
Burger King – Founded in Miami, originally as Insta-Burger King
Taco Bell – Glen Bell opens the first location in California
Subway – Opened originally as 'Pete\'s Super Submarines'
Wendy's – Dave Thomas opens the first restaurant in Ohio
Starbucks – The first store opens at Seattle's Pike Place Market
Superman – Debuts in Action Comics #1
Batman – Debuts in Detective Comics #27
Wonder Woman – Debuts in All Star Comics #8
Spider-Man – Debuts in Amazing Fantasy #15
Wolverine – Debuts in a cameo in The Incredible Hulk #180
Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles – Debuts in a self-published comic
Deadpool – Debuts in The New Mutants #98
The Teddy Bear – Created and named after President 'Teddy' Roosevelt
The Slinky – Invented by a naval engineer and hits store shelves
Mr. Potato Head – The first toy advertised on television
Barbie – Ruth Handler's iconic fashion doll debuts at the toy fair
Hot Wheels – Mattel introduces the ultimate die-cast toy cars
Rubik's Cube – Invented by Hungarian architecture professor Ernő Rubik
Furby – The robotic electronic pet becomes a massive holiday craze
SixDegrees.com – Widely considered the very first social media site
Friendster – Launched as one of the first major global social networks
Facebook – Mark Zuckerberg launches 'TheFacebook' at Harvard
YouTube – The first video, 'Me at the zoo,' is uploaded
Twitter – Jack Dorsey sends the first tweet: 'just setting up my twttr'
Instagram – The photo-sharing app launches exclusively on iOS
TikTok – Launched internationally by ByteDance
Oklahoma! – Rodgers and Hammerstein's groundbreaking first collaboration
West Side Story – Stephen Sondheim and Leonard Bernstein's tragic romance debuts
Hair – The revolutionary rock musical premieres
A Chorus Line – The definitive backstage musical opens on Broadway
The Phantom of the Opera – Andrew Lloyd Webber's masterpiece opens in the West End
Rent – Jonathan Larson's rock musical revitalizes Broadway
Hamilton – Lin-Manuel Miranda's historical hip-hop musical premieres
Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs – Disney's first full-length animated feature
Cinderella – Disney bounces back after WWII with a massive fairy tale hit
The Little Mermaid – The film that kicked off the Disney Renaissance
Toy Story – Pixar releases the first entirely computer-animated feature film
Shrek – DreamWorks turns the traditional fairy tale upside down
Finding Nemo – Pixar takes audiences on an epic underwater journey
Frozen – Elsa and Anna take the world by storm
First Network Email – Ray Tomlinson sends the first message using the '@' symbol
First Domain Registered – 'symbolics.com' becomes the first registered .com domain
World Wide Web Invented – Tim Berners-Lee writes the initial proposal at CERN
First Webcam – Created at Cambridge University just to monitor a coffee pot
First eBay Sale – Founder Pierre Omidyar sells a broken laser pointer
First Wikipedia Article – The crowdsourced encyclopedia officially launches
First Bitcoin Transaction – Satoshi Nakamoto sends 10 BTC to Hal Finney
Oreo – Nabisco introduces the 'Oreo Biscuit' to the market
Ritz Crackers – Introduced as a buttery, circular cracker during the Great Depression
Cheetos – Invented by Fritos creator Charles Elmer Doolin
Pop-Tarts – Kellogg's introduces the legendary toaster pastry
Doritos – Originally created at a restaurant in Disneyland and released nationwide
Pringles – Procter & Gamble introduces 'Pringle\'s Newfangled Potato Chips'
Lunchables – Oscar Mayer launches the pre-packaged, build-your-own lunch
The Tale of Peter Rabbit – Beatrix Potter publishes her famous tale
Winnie-the-Pooh – A. A. Milne publishes the first collection of Pooh stories
Charlotte's Web – E. B. White publishes the classic farmyard tale
The Cat in the Hat – Dr. Seuss revolutionizes beginner reading books
Where the Wild Things Are – Maurice Sendak publishes his wildly imaginative book
The Very Hungry Caterpillar – Eric Carle introduces the hungry, hole-punching bug
Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone – J.K. Rowling launches the wizarding world
Pong – Atari releases the wildly successful arcade table-tennis game
Pac-Man – Namco's yellow dot-munching mascot hits the arcades
Super Mario Bros. – Mario and Luigi save the Mushroom Kingdom on the NES
The Legend of Zelda – Link's first top-down adventure in Hyrule is released
Doom – id Software popularizes the first-person shooter genre
Final Fantasy VII – Square brings massive 3D cinematic RPGs to the PlayStation
Halo: Combat Evolved – Master Chief debuts alongside the original Xbox
Disneyland – Walt Disney opens his original park in Anaheim, California
Six Flags Over Texas – The first-ever Six Flags park opens in Arlington
Walt Disney World (Magic Kingdom) – The massive Florida resort opens its doors
Epcot – Disney's vision of a permanent World's Fair opens
Universal Studios Florida – Universal opens a theme park to rival Disney in Orlando
Disney's Animal Kingdom – Disney blends a zoo with high-tech theme park rides
The Wizarding World of Harry Potter – The immersive Hogsmeade land opens at Universal
Ford Model T – Henry Ford introduces the first affordable, mass-produced automobile
Volkswagen Beetle – Production begins on the iconic 'Bug'
Chevrolet Corvette – The quintessential American sports car is introduced
Ford Mustang – The original 'pony car' makes a wildly successful debut
Toyota Prius – The world's first mass-produced hybrid passenger car goes on sale
Tesla Model S – Tesla releases its revolutionary luxury all-electric sedan
Ford F-150 Lightning – Ford launches the all-electric version of its best-selling truck
The Microwave Oven – Percy Spencer accidentally discovers microwave cooking using radar tech
The Credit Card – Diners Club introduces the first modern multipurpose charge card
The Barcode – Norman Joseph Woodland and Bernard Silver receive the patent
The Computer Mouse – Douglas Engelbart publicly demonstrates the device for the first time
The Digital Camera – Steven Sasson at Kodak creates the first digital camera
The Compact Disc (CD) – Commercially released by Sony and Philips
The Smartphone – The IBM Simon goes on sale, featuring apps and a touchscreen
